blocks.content: He was quiet for a long time.

She watched his face and couldn't fully read it — which was unusual with him. He was one of those rare people whose expressions were legible, who didn't perform calm. But right now he was simply still.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Okay.

She blinked. She'd expected more than that. A question, maybe, or the particular gentleness that was actually another kind of pressure — the kind that opened into what do you mean, tell me more, you can trust me.
He didn't.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm not asking you to explain it today. I want you to know that whatever it is — it doesn't change what I'm doing here.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You say that.
Not unkindly. Just honestly. The way she always was.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: You don't know what you're saying it about yet.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I know.
He said it without apology.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: I'm saying it anyway, because I mean it anyway. Can I ask one thing?
She nodded.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Are you okay? Right now. Not the whole story — just today.
She considered that honestly. Not the reflexive yes, not the reassurance she'd given a hundred times before. The real answer.

blocks.speaker: Cassandra
blocks.content: Yes. Right now, yes.

blocks.speaker: Ethan
blocks.content: Then we're fine.
Something in her — the held thing, the braced thing — released. Just slightly. Just enough. The way a breath goes when you've been holding it so long you stopped noticing.
She looked at him for a long moment.
Then she picked up the brush.
